headlines.title: Why did Starmer resign and what could happen next?
headlines.description: The Labour leader says he will remain prime minister until his successor is chosen.

headlines.title: Watch and read Starmer's resignation speech in full
headlines.description: Sir Keir announces he will be step down as Labour leader and PM in a Downing Street address.

headlines.title: Top lawyer whose 'Mr Rules' approach failed to connect with the public
headlines.description: Less than two years after he won a huge majority in the 2024 general election, Sir Keir Starmer has been forced out.

headlines.title: Who is Andy Burnham? Ex-Manchester mayor who wants to lead the country
headlines.description: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor is lining up a third attempt to be Labour leader following his return to Westminster.

headlines.title: Starmer's premiership in six charts
headlines.description: BBC Verify looks at the record of Sir Keir's time in government in six key areas since he took office in July 2024.

headlines.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
headlines.description: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

headlines.title: Paedophile nursery worker could have been stopped sooner, says former colleague
headlines.description: Staff member who raised concerns about nursery worker Nathan Bennett was "ignored" for months.

headlines.title: Injured ticket inspector who helped others in Bedford train crash hailed a hero
headlines.description: A passenger praises a member of railway staff who, despite being in pain, helped the injured.

headlines.title: Lottery players urged to check tickets with £12m prize still unclaimed
headlines.description: A Lotto Jackpot ticket worth £12m bought in Rhondda Cynon Taf has yet to be claimed.

headlines.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
headlines.description: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

headlines.title: Schools close as more than half of France under red heat alerts
headlines.description: The health minister warns many citizens "will suffer", with temperatures set to peak on Monday.

headlines.title: Late singer Oliver Tree's body returned to US after helicopter crash
headlines.description: The 32-year-old California native died whilst on his world tour, ahead of a date in Brazil.

headlines.title: Boy, 3, no longer critical after crocodile attack
headlines.description: The boy's condition "is no longer critical", police say.

headlines.title: Sturgeon and Gove to team up for reality TV 'wargame'
headlines.description: The former first minister is to serve as deputy prime minister in a simulation of a Russian attack on the UK.

headlines.title: How England's Madueke went from rejection by club fans to World Cup starter
headlines.description: Noni Madueke has gone from fans protesting against him signing for Arsenal to England World Cup starter. BBC Sport look at his rollercoaster season.

headlines.title: Iran leaves note about peace in dressing room after Belgium draw
headlines.description: Iran leave a message saying "may peace, respect and friendship prevail among all nations" following their World Cup group game with Belgium.

headlines.title: A defender's view on how to stop the World Cup's best strikers
headlines.description: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland and Harry Kane look to be on lethal form in this World Cup. So how can you stop them in their tracks?

headlines.title: Salah's World Cup pain ends as he fires Egypt to historic win
headlines.description: Mohamed Salah fires Egypt to their first ever World Cup win - 92 years on from their tournament debut.

headlines.title: One of World Cup's great stories - can Cape Verde become legends?
headlines.description: Cape Verde's impressive World Cup continues with a draw against two-time winners Uruguay - and they will now be looking to make the last 32.

headlines.title: Kids were made to eat dog biscuits off the floor - pupils speak out after school abuse payouts
headlines.description: Former pupils describe staff pelting them with footballs and pouring pints of blackcurrant over them.

headlines.title: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
headlines.description: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

headlines.title: Six ways to keep your home and yourself cool in hot weather
headlines.description: Six simple things you can do to help keep your house cool when temperatures rise.

headlines.title: The picturesque mountain village that helped send Jannik Sinner to tennis stardom
headlines.description: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.

headlines.title: The remote town with no bank - and the nearest is an hour away
headlines.description: When 84-year-old Maggie Dodd discovered that the last remaining bank in Lochgilphead was closing, she began to panic.

headlines.title: Do esports students do more than play games?
headlines.description: Marketing, nutrition and video editing, there is apparently more to esports than being good at games.

headlines.title: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
headlines.description: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

headlines.title: EasyJet says US bidder trying to buy it 'on the cheap' as it rejects £4.7bn offer
headlines.description: The airline describes the latest bid approach from Castlelake as "highly opportunistic".

headlines.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
headlines.description: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

headlines.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
headlines.description: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

headlines.title: Three dead in Philippines high school shooting over bullying 'grudge'
headlines.description: Police say they believe the attack was motivated by a "grudge" over bullying.

headlines.title: Johnny Marr to auction guitars he played with The Smiths and Billie Eilish
headlines.description: The Smiths musician says he's selling his collection to stop his studio from becoming a museum.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Why Keir Starmer’s resignation looks more likely than ever
headlines.description: The government message about the PM’s future has changed.

headlines.title: Jill Biden reveals how she really felt about Joe Biden’s health whilst he was president
headlines.description: And Trump wants to give America’s top legal job to his former personal lawyer

headlines.title: Madueke's remarkable season - from petition to World Cup starter
headlines.description: Noni Madueke has gone from fans protesting against him signing for Arsenal to England World Cup starter. BBC Sport look at his rollercoaster season.

headlines.description: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.

headlines.title: Iran leave note asking for peace after Belgium draw
headlines.description: Iran leave a message saying "may peace, respect and friendship prevail among all nations" following their World Cup group game with Belgium.

headlines.title: How football united behind Doku after childbirth criticism
headlines.description: Jeremy Doku wants to fly home to attend the birth of his first child - but why has that proved controversial in some quarters?

headlines.title: 'He's stored in a hard disk in my head' - watching the World Cup as a manager
headlines.description: Former Tottenham and Brentford manager Thomas Frank on watching new players at the World Cup - who would he sign and who will be the star of the tournament?

headlines.title: US Open shows why golf is on guard against bad behaviour
headlines.description: With unruly behaviour both on and off the course during last week's US Open, it is imperative officials continue to closely police the situation, writes Iain Carter.

headlines.title: Williams is the greatest - but will she produce another great fight?
headlines.description: Serena Williams will return to the singles court at Wimbledon aged 44 - BBC Sport analyses if she is ready and the challenges she will face.
